---
title: "A spread of simulations"
subtitle: "One engine, many living systems — hover a title to read its spec"
resources:
  - gallery/
---

Each clip below is one `spec.yaml` over the identical operators and schedule grammar;
**hover (or tab to) a simulation's title to read the exact spec that generated it.**
The first two are validated to floating-point precision against
[ParticleGraph](https://github.com/allierc/ParticleGraph).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## How a clip is made
`Plexus_Main.py -o generate_plot <config>` runs the forward simulation, writes the
trajectory, and renders the movie — no per-simulation code, only the spec differs.
:::

```{=html}
<style>
.sim-gallery{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(220px,1fr));gap:1.1rem;margin:1rem 0 2rem}
.sim-card{margin:0}
.sim-card video{width:100%;border-radius:7px;background:#000;display:block;aspect-ratio:1/1;object-fit:cover}
.sim-card figcaption{margin-top:.35rem;line-height:1.25}
.sim-name{font-weight:600;cursor:help;position:relative;border-bottom:1px dotted #999}
.sim-cap{display:block;font-size:.84em;color:#777;margin-top:.1rem}
.sim-spec{display:none;position:absolute;left:0;top:1.5em;z-index:40;width:max(300px,108%);max-height:360px;overflow:auto;
  background:#1e1e1e;border-radius:7px;box-shadow:0 8px 28px rgba(0,0,0,.4);padding:.5rem .7rem;text-align:left;cursor:auto}
.sim-name:hover .sim-spec,.sim-name:focus .sim-spec{display:block}
.sim-spec pre{margin:0;font-size:.72em;line-height:1.3;white-space:pre;color:#e9e9e9;background:none;border:none;padding:0}
</style>
<h2>Attraction–repulsion &amp; boids — validated against ParticleGraph</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/arbitrary_3.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">arbitrary_3<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Full match to ParticleGraph arbitrary_3_1: 3 types, 30000 particles, sigma=0.005,
# max_radius=0.075, periodic, first_derivative, dt=0.1, 250 frames. Now feasible
# because attraction_repulsion runs on a radius neighbour graph (O(E)), not dense.
general:
  name: arbitrary_3
  seed: 0
  n_frames: 250
  dt: 0.1
  boundary: periodic

sets:
  particle:
    n: 30000
    types:
      t0: {fraction: 0.34, p: [1.6233, 1.0413, 1.6012, 1.5615]}
      t1: {fraction: 0.33, p: [1.7667, 1.8308, 1.0855, 1.9055]}
      t2: {fraction: 0.33, p: [1.7226, 1.7850, 1.0584, 1.8579]}

fields: {}

operators:
  - {op: radius_graph,         at: particle, radius: 0.075}
  - {op: attraction_repulsion, at: particle, sigma: 0.005}

schedule: [radius_graph, attraction_repulsion]

plotting:                      # render STYLE (read by plexus.plot; engine ignores it)
  background: black
  colormap: tab10
  point_size: 2.0</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">3 types, 30k particles; per-type difference-of-Gaussians law (reproduces PDE_A)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/boids_16.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">boids_16<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Exact reproduction of ParticleGraph boids reference graphs_data/boids/boids:
# 1792 particles, 16 types (112 each), 4000 frames, min_radius=0.001,
# max_radius=0.04, dt=0.5, periodic, 2nd_derivative, mean aggregation.
# Per-type p = [cohesion, alignment, separation].
general:
  name: boids_16
  seed: 0
  n_frames: 4000
  dt: 0.5
  boundary: periodic

sets:
  particle:
    n: 1792
    vel_init: 0.0004
    types:
      t00: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [27.600000381469727, 92.5, 48.20000076293945]}
      t01: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [32.0, 51.79999923706055, 29.799999237060547]}
      t02: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [23.600000381469727, 35.0, 13.5]}
      t03: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [3.299999952316284, 76.4000015258789, 13.0]}
      t04: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [94.0, 78.69999694824219, 30.799999237060547]}
      t05: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [81.4000015258789, 34.599998474121094, 2.200000047683716]}
      t06: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [3.0999999046325684, 40.5, 17.0]}
      t07: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [88.80000305175781, 7.199999809265137, 29.799999237060547]}
      t08: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [32.70000076293945, 76.80000305175781, 26.100000381469727]}
      t09: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [14.5, 56.79999923706055, 27.600000381469727]}
      t10: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [63.29999923706055, 99.9000015258789, 13.899999618530273]}
      t11: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [32.5, 25.200000762939453, 24.399999618530273]}
      t12: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [97.5999984741211, 56.5, 12.100000381469727]}
      t13: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [62.20000076293945, 9.100000381469727, 28.299999237060547]}
      t14: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [76.4000015258789, 52.900001525878906, 32.0]}
      t15: {fraction: 0.0625, p: [48.099998474121094, 54.400001525878906, 30.700000762939453]}

fields: {}

operators:
  - {op: radius_graph, at: particle, radius: 0.04, min_radius: 0.001}
  - {op: boids,        at: particle}

schedule: [radius_graph, boids]

plotting:
  background: black
  colormap: tab20
  point_size: 6.0</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">16 types of boid; cohesion/alignment/separation (reproduces PDE_B)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>Slime mould — Physarum networks</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_default.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_default<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_default -- one species, the canonical Physarum transport network.
# Port of SebLague/Slime-Simulation default settings: agents spawned in a disc at
# centre, sense 3 sensors, steer toward the strongest trail, deposit + diffuse +
# decay -&gt; a self-reinforcing network that grows and coarsens (stigmergy).
general: {name: slime_default, seed: 0, n_frames: 400, record_every: 4, dt: 1.0, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 12000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.3
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.45, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.025, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 220, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.012, diffuse: 0.35, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">self-reinforcing transport network</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_curly.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_curly<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_curly -- wide sensor cone + high turn -&gt; curly, restless, foam-like texture.
general: {name: slime_curly, seed: 0, n_frames: 400, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 13000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.3
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.005, turn_speed: 0.9, sensor_angle: 45, sensor_dist: 0.02, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 220, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.014, diffuse: 0.35, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">high turning rate, curly filaments</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_filaments.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_filaments<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_filaments -- narrow sensor cone + low turn -&gt; long, straight, branching filaments.
general: {name: slime_filaments, seed: 0, n_frames: 450, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 13000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.3
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.007, turn_speed: 0.18, sensor_angle: 14, sensor_dist: 0.035, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 220, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.01, diffuse: 0.3, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">fine filamentary structure</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_coarse_stream.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_coarse_stream<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_coarse -- long sensor reach -&gt; few large transport cells (coarse network).
general: {name: slime_coarse, seed: 0, n_frames: 400, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 12000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.3
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.008, turn_speed: 0.4, sensor_angle: 22, sensor_dist: 0.05, sensor_size: 2}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 200, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.008, diffuse: 0.4, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">coarse network, streamline view</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_fine_graph.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_fine_graph<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_fine -- short sensor reach + low diffusion -&gt; a fine, dense capillary mesh.
general: {name: slime_fine, seed: 0, n_frames: 400, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 16000
    spawn: disc
    spawn_radius: 0.32
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.004, turn_speed: 0.5, sensor_angle: 26, sensor_dist: 0.012, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 260, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.018, diffuse: 0.15, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">fine network, extracted as a graph</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_two_repel.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_two_repel<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_two_repel -- TWO species, identical rules, cross=-1 (attract own channel,
# repel the other). Categorical separation: same operator, two `types`, two field
# channels -&gt; the two slimes carve out separate territories with a sharp interface.
# Density kept BELOW field saturation (lower deposit, higher decay) so a gradient
# survives -- saturated trails are flat and the repulsion would have nothing to climb.
general: {name: slime_two_repel, seed: 0, n_frames: 420, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 11000
    spawn: random
    types:
      a: {fraction: 0.5, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
      b: {fraction: 0.5, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 240, deposit: 0.5, decay: 0.03, diffuse: 0.28, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
    b: [1.0, 0.35, 0.45]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">two species repel: separate territories, sharp interface</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_six.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_six<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_six -- SIX mutually-repelling species (cross=-1): six colour territories.
# More channels overlap (and additive-blend toward white) faster, so deposit drops
# and decay rises vs four to keep the field below saturation and the colours distinct.
general: {name: slime_six, seed: 0, n_frames: 420, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 18000
    spawn: random
    types:
      a: {fraction: 0.16667, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
      b: {fraction: 0.16667, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
      c: {fraction: 0.16667, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
      d: {fraction: 0.16667, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
      e: {fraction: 0.16666, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
      f: {fraction: 0.16666, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.032, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 240, deposit: 0.38, decay: 0.042, diffuse: 0.25, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.20, 0.95, 0.65]
    b: [1.00, 0.35, 0.45]
    c: [0.40, 0.65, 1.00]
    d: [1.00, 0.85, 0.30]
    e: [0.95, 0.40, 0.95]
    f: [1.00, 0.60, 0.20]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">six sources</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_eight.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_eight<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_eight -- EIGHT mutually-repelling species (cross=-1): eight colour territories.
# The framework is channel-agnostic -- one type = one trail channel = one colour, so
# going 4 -&gt; 8 species is purely more `types` + more `plotting.colors`, no code change.
# Lowest deposit / highest decay / most agents of the suite: eight overlapping channels
# saturate (and additive-blend to white) fastest, so the density is the most restrained.
general: {name: slime_eight, seed: 0, n_frames: 440, record_every: 4, boundary: wall}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 24000
    spawn: random
    types:
      a: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      b: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      c: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      d: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      e: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      f: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      g: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
      h: {fraction: 0.125, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.42, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.034, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 256, deposit: 0.33, decay: 0.05, diffuse: 0.24, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.20, 0.95, 0.65]
    b: [1.00, 0.35, 0.45]
    c: [0.40, 0.65, 1.00]
    d: [1.00, 0.85, 0.30]
    e: [0.95, 0.40, 0.95]
    f: [1.00, 0.60, 0.20]
    g: [0.20, 0.85, 0.85]
    h: [0.65, 0.50, 1.00]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">eight sources</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slime_torus.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slime_torus<span class="sim-spec"><pre># slime_torus -- periodic (wrap-around) boundary: an endless, seamless network with
# no walls to reflect off. Tests the engine&#x27;s periodic boundary path.
general: {name: slime_torus, seed: 0, n_frames: 400, record_every: 4, boundary: periodic}
sets:
  cell:
    n: 18000
    spawn: random
    types:
      a: {fraction: 1.0, move_speed: 0.006, turn_speed: 0.45, sensor_angle: 28, sensor_dist: 0.025, sensor_size: 1}
fields:
  trail: {frame: trail, res: 240, deposit: 0.9, decay: 0.012, diffuse: 0.35, couples_to: cell}
operators:
- {op: slime, at: cell, from: trail, to: trail, cross: -1.0}
schedule: [slime, trail.diffuse]
plotting:
  colors:
    a: [0.2, 0.95, 0.65]
  background: black
  gamma: 0.7</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">periodic (toroidal) domain</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>MPM fluids &amp; materials</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mat_liquid.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mat_liquid<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: mat_liquid
seed: 0
n_frames: 500
record_every: 2
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5, 0.80, 0.5, 0.80]
    types:
      ball: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 200, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 80, material: liquid}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 2200, radius: 0.11, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 12, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.1, wall_damp: 0.9, surface_tension: 4000.0}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">liquid material (no shear memory)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mat_elastic.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mat_elastic<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: mat_elastic
seed: 0
n_frames: 500
record_every: 2
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5, 0.80, 0.5, 0.80]
    types:
      ball: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 200, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 200, material: elastic}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 2200, radius: 0.11, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 12, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.1, wall_damp: 0.9}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">elastic material (shape memory)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mat_snow.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mat_snow<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: mat_snow
seed: 0
n_frames: 500
record_every: 2
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5, 0.80, 0.5, 0.80]
    types:
      ball: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 200, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 200, material: snow}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 2200, radius: 0.11, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 12, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.1, wall_damp: 0.9}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">snow / granular material</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ph_crown_splash.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ph_crown_splash<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: ph_crown_splash
seed: 0
n_frames: 900
record_every: 5
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 2
    start: [0.5,0.86,0.5,0.86]
    types:
      water: {fraction: 0.5, youngs: 300, block: [0.03,0.03,0.97,0.38], layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 300, material: liquid}]}
      ball: {fraction: 0.5, youngs: 500, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 500, material: elastic}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 7000, radius: 0.10, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 18, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.1, wall_damp: 0.5, wall_contact: 0.06, surface_tension: 10}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">a drop hits a pool: crown splash</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ph_coalesce.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ph_coalesce<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: ph_coalesce
seed: 0
n_frames: 500
record_every: 5
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 2
    start: [0.5,0.54,0.5,0.54]
    types:
      a: {fraction: 0.5, youngs: 200, block: [0.30,0.44,0.50,0.64], layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 200, material: liquid}]}
      b: {fraction: 0.5, youngs: 200, block: [0.50,0.44,0.70,0.64], layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 200, material: liquid}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 3500, radius: 0.24, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 0.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 18, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.12, wall_damp: 1.0, wall_contact: 0.06, surface_tension: 30}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">two drops coalesce under surface tension</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ph_slosh.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ph_slosh<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: ph_slosh
seed: 0
n_frames: 1000
record_every: 5
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5,0.2,0.5,0.2]
    types:
      water: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 300, block: [0.05,0.04,0.95,0.30], layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 300, material: liquid}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 9000, radius: 0.30, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0, gx: 6.0, gy: -10.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 18, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.15, wall_damp: 0.5, wall_contact: 0.06, surface_tension: 10}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">sloshing in a vessel</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ob_dam_break.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ob_dam_break<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: ob_dam_break
seed: 0
n_frames: 900
record_every: 5
boundary: wall
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.17,0.4,0.17,0.4]
    types:
      water: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 300, block: [0.04,0.03,0.30,0.78], layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 300, material: liquid}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 9000, radius: 0.30, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 18, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.08, wall_damp: 0.5, wall_contact: 0.06, surface_tension: 10}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">dam break against an obstacle</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ob_funnel.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ob_funnel<span class="sim-spec"><pre>name: ob_funnel
seed: 0
n_frames: 1000
record_every: 5
boundary: wall
obstacles:
  - [0.00,0.0,0.18,1.0]
  - [0.18,0.0,0.28,0.62]
  - [0.28,0.0,0.38,0.42]
  - [0.38,0.0,0.45,0.26]
  - [0.55,0.0,0.62,0.26]
  - [0.62,0.0,0.72,0.42]
  - [0.72,0.0,0.82,0.62]
  - [0.82,0.0,1.0,1.0]
sets:
  cell:
    n: 1
    start: [0.5,0.8,0.5,0.8]
    types:
      water: {fraction: 1.0, youngs: 300, layers: [{frac: 1.0, youngs: 300, material: liquid}]}
  particle: {parent: cell, per_parent: 7000, radius: 0.20, density: 1.0}
fields:
  chemical: {frame: grid, res: 64, diffusion: 0.1, decay: 0.0, couples_to: cell}
operators:
  - {op: gravity, at: cell, g: 12.0}
  - {op: mpm, at: particle, n_grid: 128, substeps: 18, dt_sub: 2.0e-4, a_max: 200, drag: 0.3, wall_damp: 0.5, wall_contact: 0.06, surface_tension: 14}
schedule: [aggregate, gravity, mpm]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">draining through a funnel</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>The Well — fields &amp; active matter</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/am_flock.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">am_flock<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Vicsek self-propelled particles -- the microscopic model behind the_well/active_matter
# (whose stored continuum coarse-grains exactly this). SET half of Plexus: a particle
# Level + a rewired radius graph (Rewire) + an alignment Lateral operator, the SAME
# machinery the water particles used. Only the spec changes between regimes.
# low noise -&gt; global alignment (ordered polar flock; Vicsek ordered phase)
name: am_flock
seed: 0
n_frames: 600
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
sets:
  active: {n: 4000}
operators:
  - {op: radius_graph, at: active, radius: 0.05}
  - {op: active_matter, at: active, v0: 0.010, noise: 0.04}
schedule: [radius_graph, active_matter]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">active matter: a coherent polar flock (Vicsek)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/rd_worms.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">rd_worms<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion -- worms regime.
# the_well/gray_scott_reaction_diffusion (Ohana et al., NeurIPS 2024). One FIELD
# self-update operator; only (f,k) and the initial condition change vs the others.
# Worms: elongating, branching filaments.
name: rd_worms
seed: 0
n_frames: 7000
record_every: 70
world: 1.0
fields:
  chem: {frame: grid, res: 128, channels: 2, bc: periodic}
init:
  chem:
    A: 1.0
    B: {seed: clusters, n: 30, radius: 2.0, value: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: reaction_diffusion, at: chem, f: 0.058, k: 0.065, Da: 0.16, Db: 0.08, substeps: 12}
schedule: [reaction_diffusion]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: Gray–Scott reaction–diffusion, worms</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/wave_double_slit.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">wave_double_slit<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Classic double-slit DIFFRACTION: a pressure pulse below a barrier passes through
# two narrow gaps and the two transmitted wavefronts interfere above. Sound-hard
# barrier via the obstacle mask (the_well acoustic family).
name: wave_double_slit
seed: 0
n_frames: 520
record_every: 4
world: 1.0
fields:
  acoustic:
    frame: grid
    res: 256
    channels: 3
    bc_x: wall
    bc_y: open
    obstacles:
    - [0.0, 0.49, 0.31999999999999995, 0.51]
    - [0.38, 0.49, 0.62, 0.51]
    - [0.68, 0.49, 1.0, 0.51]
init:
  acoustic:
    rho: {kind: uniform, value: 1.0}
    p: {seed: point, cx: 0.5, cy: 0.22, r: 0.018, inten: 2.5}
operators:
- {op: wave_acoustic, at: acoustic, K: 4.0, cfl: 0.3, substeps: 6, sponge: 0.12}
schedule: [wave_acoustic]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: acoustic double-slit diffraction</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/wave_helmholtz_staircase.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">wave_helmholtz_staircase<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Wave scattering off a periodic STAIRCASE -&gt; the_well/helmholtz_staircase. Reuses
# the SAME wave_acoustic operator; only the obstacle geometry changes (spec-is-API).
name: wave_helmholtz_staircase
seed: 0
n_frames: 640
record_every: 4
world: 1.0
fields:
  acoustic:
    frame: grid
    res: 256
    channels: 3
    bc_x: wall
    bc_y: open
    obstacles:
    - [0.0, 0.0, 0.14285714285714285, 0.12857142857142856]
    - [0.14285714285714285, 0.0, 0.2857142857142857, 0.2571428571428571]
    - [0.2857142857142857, 0.0, 0.42857142857142855, 0.3857142857142857]
    - [0.42857142857142855, 0.0, 0.5714285714285714, 0.5142857142857142]
    - [0.5714285714285714, 0.0, 0.7142857142857143, 0.6428571428571429]
    - [0.7142857142857143, 0.0, 0.8571428571428571, 0.7714285714285714]
    - [0.8571428571428571, 0.0, 1.0, 0.9]
init:
  acoustic:
    rho: {kind: uniform, value: 1.0}
    p: {seed: point, cx: 0.45, cy: 0.82, r: 0.018, inten: 2.5}
operators:
- {op: wave_acoustic, at: acoustic, K: 4.0, cfl: 0.3, substeps: 6, sponge: 0.12}
schedule: [wave_acoustic]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: waves scattering off a periodic staircase</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/wave_lens.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">wave_lens<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Acoustic focusing: a single slow (high-rho) disc acts as a converging lens for a
# plane-ish pulse. Heterogeneous-medium wave propagation (the_well acoustic family).
name: wave_lens
seed: 3
n_frames: 520
record_every: 4
world: 1.0
fields:
  acoustic: {frame: grid, res: 256, channels: 3, bc_x: wall, bc_y: open}
init:
  acoustic:
    rho: {kind: lens, cx: 0.5, cy: 0.62, r: 0.16, rho: 7.0}
    p: {seed: rings, n: 1}
operators:
  - {op: wave_acoustic, at: acoustic, K: 4.0, cfl: 0.30, substeps: 6, sponge: 0.12}
schedule: [wave_acoustic]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: acoustic waves focused by a lens</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ns_rayleigh_benard.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ns_rayleigh_benard<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Rayleigh-Benard convection -&gt; the_well/rayleigh_benard. Hot (buoyant) fluid under
# cold; body_force(buoyancy) drives rolls. Schedule = buoyancy + advect + diffuse +
# project + advect(T). This is what advect+poisson_solve unlock together.
name: ns_rayleigh_benard
seed: 2
n_frames: 700
record_every: 4
world: 1.0
fields: {fluid: {frame: grid, res: 256, channels: 4, bc: periodic}}
init: {fluid: {preset: rayleigh_benard, seed: 0.03, width: 0.05}}
operators:
  - {op: body_force, at: fluid, scalar: 2, g: 28.0, ref: 0.5, dt_sub: 0.006}
  - {op: advect, at: fluid, vel: [0,1], channels: [0,1], dt_sub: 0.006, substeps: 2}
  - {op: diffuse, at: fluid, channels: [0,1], D: 0.00004, dt_sub: 0.012}
  - {op: poisson_solve, at: fluid, vel: [0,1]}
  - {op: advect, at: fluid, vel: [0,1], channels: [2,3], dt_sub: 0.006, substeps: 2}
  - {op: diffuse, at: fluid, channels: [2], D: 0.00002, dt_sub: 0.012}
schedule: [body_force, advect, diffuse, poisson_solve, advect, diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: Rayleigh–Bénard convection rolls (Navier–Stokes)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ns_vortices.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ns_vortices<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Decaying 2D turbulence: six random vortices merge and form coherent structures
# (the inverse cascade). advect + project only.
name: ns_vortices
seed: 4
n_frames: 520
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields: {fluid: {frame: grid, res: 256, channels: 4, bc: periodic}}
init: {fluid: {preset: vortices, n: 8, bands: 8}}
operators:
  - {op: advect, at: fluid, vel: [0,1], channels: [0,1,2,3], dt_sub: 0.005, substeps: 2}
  - {op: diffuse, at: fluid, channels: [0,1], D: 0.00002, dt_sub: 0.01}
  - {op: poisson_solve, at: fluid, vel: [0,1]}
schedule: [advect, diffuse, poisson_solve]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: decaying 2-D turbulence, vortices merge (Navier–Stokes)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ns_taylor_green.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ns_taylor_green<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Taylor-Green vortex decay -- the canonical incompressible-NS test (vortex lattice
# that cascades to small scales). Pure advect + project, viscous decay.
name: ns_taylor_green
seed: 0
n_frames: 420
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields: {fluid: {frame: grid, res: 256, channels: 4, bc: periodic}}
init: {fluid: {preset: taylor_green, U: 0.8}}
operators:
  - {op: advect, at: fluid, vel: [0,1], channels: [0,1,2,3], dt_sub: 0.004, substeps: 2}
  - {op: diffuse, at: fluid, channels: [0,1], D: 0.00004, dt_sub: 0.008}
  - {op: poisson_solve, at: fluid, vel: [0,1]}
schedule: [advect, diffuse, poisson_solve]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: Taylor–Green vortex decay (Navier–Stokes)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/ns_rayleigh_taylor.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">ns_rayleigh_taylor<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Rayleigh-Taylor instability -&gt; the_well/rayleigh_taylor_instability. Heavy fluid
# above light; body_force with g&lt;0 makes it sink into fingers/mushrooms.
name: ns_rayleigh_taylor
seed: 3
n_frames: 700
record_every: 4
world: 1.0
fields: {fluid: {frame: grid, res: 256, channels: 4, bc: periodic}}
init: {fluid: {preset: rayleigh_taylor, amp: 0.012, k: 3}}
operators:
  - {op: body_force, at: fluid, scalar: 2, g: -26.0, ref: 0.5, dt_sub: 0.006}
  - {op: advect, at: fluid, vel: [0,1], channels: [0,1], dt_sub: 0.006, substeps: 2}
  - {op: diffuse, at: fluid, channels: [0,1], D: 0.00003, dt_sub: 0.012}
  - {op: poisson_solve, at: fluid, vel: [0,1]}
  - {op: advect, at: fluid, vel: [0,1], channels: [2,3], dt_sub: 0.006, substeps: 2}
schedule: [body_force, advect, diffuse, poisson_solve, advect]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">field: Rayleigh–Taylor fingering instability (Navier–Stokes)</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>Mixtures — several mechanisms in one world</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mix_avoid.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mix_avoid<span class="sim-spec"><pre># MIX (2 families): same worms morphogen, but NEGATIVE chemotaxis gain -&gt; particles
# FLEE the ridges and pack into the valleys (one sign flip in the spec, opposite behaviour).
name: mix_avoid
seed: 1
n_frames: 600
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields: {chem: {frame: grid, res: 128, channels: 2, bc: periodic}}
sets: {active: {n: 3000}}
init:
  chem: {A: 1.0, B: {seed: clusters, n: 18, radius: 2.0, value: 1.0}}
operators:
  - {op: reaction_diffusion, at: chem, f: 0.058, k: 0.065, substeps: 12}
  - {op: radius_graph, at: active, radius: 0.05}
  - {op: active_matter, at: active, v0: 0.004, noise: 0.12}
  - {op: chemotaxis, at: active, from_field: chem, channel: 1, gain: -0.0022}
schedule: [reaction_diffusion, radius_graph, active_matter, chemotaxis]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">particles flee a reaction–diffusion ridge field</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mix_slime4.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mix_slime4<span class="sim-spec"><pre># MIX: four-species SLIME (Physarum) + ACTIVE-MATTER alignment, PAIRED trail affinity.
# Four particle types, each laying its own trail channel. The affinity matrix W[s,:]
# sets which trails a species follows: pairs (0,1) and (2,3) share trails, the two
# pairs repel -&gt; four types running two coupled transport NETWORKS.
#   trail_diffuse -&gt; 4-channel pheromone field self-update (diffuse + evaporate)   FIELD
#   radius_graph  -&gt; neighbour relation for the alignment term                     REWIRE
#   slime         -&gt; sense weighted trail (3 sensors) + Vicsek alignment + move +  EXCHANGE
#                    deposit. The Jones/Lague steer builds networks; the alignment
#                    term IS the active-matter half, fused on the shared heading.
name: mix_slime4
seed: 0
n_frames: 560
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields:
  trail: {frame: grid, res: 200, channels: 4, bc: periodic}
sets:
  active: {n: 8000, species: 4}
operators:
  - {op: trail_diffuse, at: trail, D: 0.13, decay: 0.04, substeps: 1}
  - {op: radius_graph, at: active, radius: 0.02}
  - {op: slime, at: active, from_field: trail, to_field: trail, v0: 0.004,
     turn: 0.5, sensor_angle: 24, sensor_dist: 0.022, align: 0.10, noise: 0.06,
     amount: 0.05, cap: 1.0,
     affinity: [[1.0, 0.9, -0.5, -0.5],
                [0.9, 1.0, -0.5, -0.5],
                [-0.5, -0.5, 1.0, 0.9],
                [-0.5, -0.5, 0.9, 1.0]]}
schedule: [trail_diffuse, radius_graph, slime]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">four slime species run two coupled networks</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mix_taxis_maze_A.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mix_taxis_maze_A<span class="sim-spec"><pre># MIX (2 families): Gray-Scott MAZE morphogen + particles tracing its corridors.
name: mix_taxis_maze
seed: 3
n_frames: 700
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields: {chem: {frame: grid, res: 128, channels: 2, bc: periodic}}
sets: {active: {n: 3500}}
init:
  chem: {A: 1.0, B: {seed: fourier, kmax: 8, thresh: 0.55, value: 1.0}}
operators:
  - {op: reaction_diffusion, at: chem, f: 0.029, k: 0.057, substeps: 12}
  - {op: radius_graph, at: active, radius: 0.05}
  - {op: active_matter, at: active, v0: 0.004, noise: 0.12}
  - {op: chemotaxis, at: active, from_field: chem, channel: 1, gain: 0.0022}
schedule: [reaction_diffusion, radius_graph, active_matter, chemotaxis]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">particles trace a Gray–Scott maze morphogen</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mix_chemotaxis.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mix_chemotaxis<span class="sim-spec"><pre># MIX of TWO families: a FIELD PDE and a SET model, coupled.
#   reaction_diffusion -&gt; a Gray-Scott &quot;worms&quot; morphogen pattern (FIELD self-update)
#   active_matter      -&gt; Vicsek particles that flock                (SET) + radius_graph
#   chemotaxis         -&gt; particles climb the morphogen&#x27;s B channel  (EXCHANGE set&lt;-field)
# On the particle set the flocking and chemotaxis deltas simply SUM -- the framework&#x27;s
# &quot;several operators on one set, deltas add&quot; rule. Mixing = a longer schedule, nothing else.
name: mix_chemotaxis
seed: 1
n_frames: 600
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields:
  chem: {frame: grid, res: 128, channels: 2, bc: periodic}
sets:
  active: {n: 3000}
init:
  chem:
    A: 1.0
    B: {seed: clusters, n: 18, radius: 2.0, value: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: reaction_diffusion, at: chem, f: 0.058, k: 0.065, substeps: 12}
  - {op: radius_graph, at: active, radius: 0.05}
  - {op: active_matter, at: active, v0: 0.004, noise: 0.12}
  - {op: chemotaxis, at: active, from_field: chem, channel: 1, gain: 0.0020}
schedule: [reaction_diffusion, radius_graph, active_matter, chemotaxis]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">a Vicsek flock climbs a morphogen gradient</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mix_acoustic_push.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mix_acoustic_push<span class="sim-spec"><pre># MIX (2 families): an ACOUSTIC wave + particles pushed by its pressure gradient.
# wave_acoustic(field) + active_matter(set) coupled by the SAME chemotaxis op, just
# pointed at the pressure channel -&gt; expanding wavefronts herd the swarm.
name: mix_acoustic_push
seed: 0
n_frames: 600
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields: {acoustic: {frame: grid, res: 128, channels: 3, bc: periodic}}
sets: {active: {n: 3000}}
init:
  acoustic: {rho: {kind: uniform, value: 1.0}, p: {seed: rings, n: 3}}
operators:
  - {op: wave_acoustic, at: acoustic, K: 4.0, cfl: 0.30, substeps: 4, sponge: 0.0}
  - {op: radius_graph, at: active, radius: 0.05}
  - {op: active_matter, at: active, v0: 0.003, noise: 0.10}
  - {op: chemotaxis, at: active, from_field: acoustic, channel: 0, gain: -0.0010}
schedule: [wave_acoustic, radius_graph, active_matter, chemotaxis]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">expanding acoustic wavefronts herd a swarm</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/mix_world.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">mix_world<span class="sim-spec"><pre># MIX: all three operator families in ONE schedule, one world.
#   reaction_diffusion -&gt; a Gray-Scott morphogen pattern (FIELD)
#   wave_acoustic      -&gt; an acoustic ripple in the same domain (FIELD)
#   active_matter      -&gt; Vicsek particles that flock (SET)         + radius_graph (Rewire)
#   chemotaxis         -&gt; particles climb the morphogen&#x27;s B channel (EXCHANGE set&lt;-&gt;field)
# Flocking and chemotaxis deltas simply SUM on the particle set (the framework&#x27;s
# &#x27;several operators on one set, deltas add&#x27; rule). Mixing is just a longer schedule.
name: mix_world
seed: 1
n_frames: 600
record_every: 3
world: 1.0
fields:
  chem:     {frame: grid, res: 128, channels: 2, bc: periodic}
  acoustic: {frame: grid, res: 128, channels: 3, bc: periodic}
sets:
  active: {n: 3000}
init:
  chem:
    A: 1.0
    B: {seed: clusters, n: 18, radius: 2.0, value: 1.0}
  acoustic:
    rho: {kind: uniform, value: 1.0}
    p: {seed: rings, n: 3}
operators:
  - {op: reaction_diffusion, at: chem, f: 0.058, k: 0.065, substeps: 12}
  - {op: wave_acoustic, at: acoustic, K: 4.0, cfl: 0.30, substeps: 4, sponge: 0.0}
  - {op: radius_graph, at: active, radius: 0.05}
  - {op: active_matter, at: active, v0: 0.004, noise: 0.12}
  - {op: chemotaxis, at: active, from_field: chem, channel: 1, gain: 0.0006}
schedule: [reaction_diffusion, wave_acoustic, radius_graph, active_matter, chemotaxis]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">all three operator families coupled in one world</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>

<h2>Microswimmers</h2>
<div class="sim-gallery">
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/motile.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">motile<span class="sim-spec"><pre># A motile (swimming) ciliate: the sphere self-propels at U = (2/3) B_1 along its
# axis, sweeping through the nutrient bath and leaving a depleted wake (lab frame).
# Same registry as sessile.yaml -- only the type (lifestyle) + the swim op differ.
name: motile
seed: 0
n_frames: 200
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.6
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.12
    n_mode: 40
    axis: 0.0
    types:
      motile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: motile, feeding_area: 0.5}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 192, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 192, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.05, peclet: 120.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow, at: organism, to: flow, frame: lab}
  - {op: slip,   at: surface_node}
  - {op: swim,   at: &quot;organism[type=motile]&quot;, gain: 0.012}
  - {op: absorb, at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, swim, absorb, chemical.diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">active self-propelled swimmers</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/slipwave_motile.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">slipwave_motile<span class="sim-spec"><pre># A motile (swimming) ciliate: the sphere self-propels at U = (2/3) B_1 along its
# axis, sweeping through the nutrient bath and leaving a depleted wake (lab frame).
# Same registry as sessile.yaml -- only the type (lifestyle) + the swim op differ.
name: motile
seed: 0
n_frames: 200
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.6
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.12
    n_mode: 40
    axis: 0.0
    types:
      motile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: motile, feeding_area: 0.5}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 192, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 192, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.05, peclet: 120.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow, at: organism, to: flow, frame: lab}
  - {op: slip,   at: surface_node}
  - {op: swim,   at: &quot;organism[type=motile]&quot;, gain: 0.012}
  - {op: absorb, at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, swim, absorb, chemical.diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">motile swimmer — slip-wave field overlay</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/vorticity_motile.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">vorticity_motile<span class="sim-spec"><pre># A motile (swimming) ciliate: the sphere self-propels at U = (2/3) B_1 along its
# axis, sweeping through the nutrient bath and leaving a depleted wake (lab frame).
# Same registry as sessile.yaml -- only the type (lifestyle) + the swim op differ.
name: motile
seed: 0
n_frames: 200
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.6
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.12
    n_mode: 40
    axis: 0.0
    types:
      motile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: motile, feeding_area: 0.5}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 192, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 192, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.05, peclet: 120.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow, at: organism, to: flow, frame: lab}
  - {op: slip,   at: surface_node}
  - {op: swim,   at: &quot;organism[type=motile]&quot;, gain: 0.012}
  - {op: absorb, at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, swim, absorb, chemical.diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">motile swimmer — vorticity field overlay</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/vortzoom_fast.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">vortzoom_fast<span class="sim-spec"><pre># A motile (swimming) ciliate: the sphere self-propels at U = (2/3) B_1 along its
# axis, sweeping through the nutrient bath and leaving a depleted wake (lab frame).
# Same registry as sessile.yaml -- only the type (lifestyle) + the swim op differ.
name: motile
seed: 0
n_frames: 200
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.6
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.12
    n_mode: 40
    axis: 0.0
    types:
      motile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: motile, feeding_area: 0.5}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 192, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 192, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.05, peclet: 120.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow, at: organism, to: flow, frame: lab}
  - {op: slip,   at: surface_node}
  - {op: swim,   at: &quot;organism[type=motile]&quot;, gain: 0.012}
  - {op: absorb, at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, swim, absorb, chemical.diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">motile swimmer — vorticity close-up</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/feeding.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">feeding<span class="sim-spec"><pre># Phagotrophy: a sessile ciliate sits in a gentle ambient current (drift) that
# carries food parcels (tracers) past it left-&gt;right. Its feeding current bends the
# nearby parcels and its absorbing mouth -- here facing UPSTREAM -- EATS the ones
# that arrive. Parcels leaving the domain respawn at the left inlet, so the stream
# is continuous and the eaten count climbs.
#
# Adds one set (tracer) + two operators (advect_particles, capture) to sessile.yaml
# -- same registry, no engine change. Reference: Liu, Costello &amp; Kanso, Nat Commun
# 16, 4154 (2025).
name: feeding
seed: 0
n_frames: 360
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.6
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.14
    n_mode: 40
    axis: 0.0
    types:
      sessile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: sessile, feeding_area: 0.3, mouth_offset: 3.14159}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
  tracer: {n: 900}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 176, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 176, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.06, peclet: 120.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow,    at: organism, to: flow, frame: body}
  - {op: slip,             at: surface_node}
  - {op: absorb,           at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
  - {op: advect_particles, at: tracer, from: flow, speed: 0.04, drift: [0.006, 0.0]}
  - {op: capture,          at: tracer, margin: 0.25}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, absorb, chemical.diffuse, advect_particles, capture]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">feeding on a resource field</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure class="sim-card">
    <video src="gallery/sessile.mp4" autoplay loop muted playsinline preload="metadata"></video>
    <figcaption>
      <span class="sim-name" tabindex="0">sessile<span class="sim-spec"><pre># A sessile (attached) ciliate: anchored sphere whose cilia drive a feeding current
# over a 50%-coverage mouth cap. Reproduces the sessile column of the paper&#x27;s
# flow + concentration figures (Liu, Costello &amp; Kanso, Nat Commun 2025).
name: sessile
seed: 0
n_frames: 120
record_every: 2
dt: 1.0
world: 1.0
sets:
  organism:
    n: 1
    radius: 0.14
    n_mode: 40
    types:
      sessile: {fraction: 1.0, lifestyle: sessile, feeding_area: 0.2}
  surface_node: {parent: organism, per_parent: 240, role: membrane}
fields:
  flow:     {kind: flow, res: 160, couples_to: organism}
  chemical: {kind: chemical, res: 160, couples_to: surface_node, diffusion: 0.06, peclet: 100.0, c_inf: 1.0}
operators:
  - {op: squirmer_flow, at: organism, to: flow, frame: body}
  - {op: slip,    at: surface_node}
  - {op: absorb,  at: &quot;surface_node[type=mouth]&quot;, from: chemical, rate: 1.0}
schedule: [squirmer_flow, slip, absorb, chemical.diffuse]</pre></span></span>
      <span class="sim-cap">attached, beating cilia</span>
    </figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>
```
